QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $707.24 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw SPY slip 0.15% to 738.18, while QQQ fell 0.85% to 707.24, with the Nasdaq weighed by a 3.23% drop in SOXX. Oil stabilized near $101 after Trump rejected Iran's peace proposal—keeping the 10-week Hormuz blockade firmly in place. CPI data for April was released, coming in at a hot 3.8% YoY (per Kalshi/BLS), which kept the US10Y ticking up 5bp to 4.46% and dampened rate-cut hopes.
commodities_note: WTI edged down 0.15% to $101.02, consolidating after Monday's spike. Gold was quiet at $4,704.60. The DXY held near 98.32. QQQ's $707.24 print falls outside the expected $250-$700 range—verify source data as it may indicate a contract adjustment or a genuine historic move.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are now hard-blocked on overbought RSI and extension above the 50-day, with volume absent. UOB was downgraded to CAUTION as the RSI crossed 65, snapping its brief WATCH status. NIM compression remains structural until US10Y breaks below 4.20% or MAS eases—neither catalyst is imminent.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM pressure persists with no near-term relief catalyst.
singapore.key_development: UOB's signal downgrade highlights that the cluster's isolated technical uptick has run into overbought territory; OCBC and DBS already there.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is uniformly extended — 11 of 11 members are CAUTION, with most exceeding +20% above their 50-day. Today's 3.23% SOXX drop, led by AMD, MU, and ASML, shows the rally is finally pausing, but valuations remain stretched. NVDA's May 21 earnings is the next high-impact catalyst that could reset the picture.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — earnings and pullbacks needed before entry.
semiconductors.key_development: Broad-based sell-off in memory stocks (MU -4%, SK Hynix still surging earlier) signals the first chink in the AI memory trade; AVGO and TSM also declined.

big_tech.summary: MSFT is the lone BigTech name holding near its 50-day, while AMZN and GOOG remain deep in CAUTION territory with extreme extensions. PLTR dipped below its 50-day, clearing the hard block and moving to HOLD on a valuation reset. The cluster's fortunes are tied to capex narratives and the May 21 NVDA earnings read-through.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex optimism is priced in, and only MSFT offers a near-term technical reset.
big_tech.key_development: PLTR upgraded to HOLD as price fell below the 50-day, removing the technical block; the AI defense theme remains intact but expensive.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Sister optics play to LITE — also received $2B NVDA photonics investment. LITE's earnings this week and NVDA Blackwell ramp are direct catalysts.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from its peak, aligning with LITE's optics supply surge.

interconnected.name: Occidental Petroleum
interconnected.reason: Oil at $101 and sustained Hormuz risk makes OXY a defensive hedge; not on watchlist but relevant as a commodity proxy.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for OXY pullback to $70-72 (200-day) if WTI holds above $90 for 2+ weeks.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed (day 22); Trump rejected Iran's peace proposal, and safe passage has not been restored — OPEC output at lowest in two decades.
US-China trade war escalates with threats of new 100% tariffs and tech export limits; 125% retaliatory tariffs remain in place with exceptions, and China's rare earth restrictions persist.
Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50-3.75%, with no clear pivot timeline after hot CPI (3.8% YoY in April); US10Y rose to 4.46%, pressuring growth stocks.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's sustained >$100 level is buoying defense and energy names while compressing semis valuations against rising rates. Singapore banks' NIM compression is locked in with US10Y at 4.46%—well above the 4.20% relief threshold—and MAS is on hold until October. BigTech and semis are extended, but the AI capex boom is keeping them elevated against macro headwinds.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: April CPI released at 3.8% YoY (per Kalshi/BLS), hotter than expected, pushing US10Y to 4.46% and reinforcing the hawkish hold narrative. Trump's rejection of Iran's peace offer on Monday continues to sustain oil near $101.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new buy entries. The watchlist is overwhelmingly extended, and earnings risk (NVDA May 21, plus today's TSEM and WRD reports) adds binary uncertainty. Wait for pullbacks to key moving averages before deploying capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensively positioned — nearly the entire watchlist is mechanically blocked.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Seventeen of 21 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, most >20% above their 50-day. The only investable posture is waiting for pullbacks, with NVDA's May 21 earnings as the next potential inflection.
